comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Mathematical Review (critic-agent)

**Tracker note:** New-issue creation for `rm-09303` is rejected by the API (`{"error":"invalid problem id"}` on `POST /api/issues/rm-09303`); only this pre-seeded main issue accepts comments/docs. I therefore record all gaps here in issue-ready form. Full write-up linked as the **Critic Analysis** document.

This is a philosophy-of-science problem (Carnap/Kant quid facti? vs quid juris?, Hume, Goodman, Reichenbach) cast in amsthm notation. The attempt's high-level architecture is right: define objectivity = existence of an *inter-subjectively invariant valid justification* (O), then prove (a)/(b)/(c) and refute deflationism via three lemmas. The defects are in whether the load-bearing concepts are coherent/non-vacuous and whether the steps actually establish their claims.

### Genuine gaps

**G1 (central) — 'valid justification' undischarged; Hume leaves O possibly empty/question-begging.** *(Def. 4, Def. 7, Prop. b.)* Objectivity is defined via existence of a valid J; Def. 4 admits 'methodological postulates (e.g. a uniformity assumption)' as premises. But Hume's problem is exactly that such a postulate is either an inductive claim needing justification (regress/circularity) or a bare stipulation (only conditional warrant). The attempt never shows O is non-empty non-question-beggingly; Prop. c's 'O is non-empty whenever some procedure carries an invariant warrant' is a tautology. So (a)-(c) may be proven about an empty/incoherent set, and (b) is arguably violated by *postponing* quid juris?.

**G2 — invariance presupposes a unique 'fixed logic of support' that is itself contested.** *(Def. 6.)* Which logic of partial support is correct (Carnap λ-continuum / subjective Bayes / Popperian corroboration) is a contested quid juris? question. Fixing one and calling reasoning relative to it 'agent-invariant' risks parochialism — exactly what Prop. a(i) condemns. Owes an argument that a unique non-arbitrary logic of support exists, else invariance is relative-to-a-choice.

**G3 — internal inconsistency: Def. 1 'purely syntactic' procedures vs the grue example in Prop. a(i).** Def. 1 says Pi(h|E) depends only on 'logical and descriptive content.' Prop. a(i) claims a grue-style policy 'must cite the tribe's habits' so is non-invariant. Goodman's lesson is the opposite: green and grue are equally syntactically respectable; no purely syntactic fact distinguishes the projectible predicate. So either purely-formal procedures can't rule out grue (cell-(i) example fails) or projectibility isn't purely syntactic (contradicts Def. 1). Cannot have both.

**G4 — Prop. a conflates 'extensionally distinct/no inclusion' with 'not definable from'; cells iii/iv hand-waved.** Condition (a) is non-*definability* of O from F. The four-cells argument shows at most extensional non-identity; logical independence does not entail non-definability. Cells (iii)/(iv) are 'clearly possible' with no instance; cell (i) relies on the contested grue example. Need a direct argument that O's membership *criterion* never mentions behavioural predicates, plus explicit instances.

**G5 — Lemma 1 (self-application dilemma) begs the question; cases not exhaustive.** Case 1 is a tu quoque: inferring 'D gives outsiders no reason' assumes the agent-invariant standard of 'reason' that D denies. Case 2 assumes asserting D 'as something one ought to accept' presupposes agent-invariant warrant; a quietist/Rortyan cashes 'ought to accept' in shared-practice terms. Dichotomy not exhaustive → refutes a strawman.

**G6 — Lemma 3 (indispensability) only defeats synchronic deflationism.** The 'current practice is in error becomes incoherent' move holds only if warrant = *current* endorsement. Limit-of-inquiry pragmatism (Peirce, Wright's superassertibility) accommodates 'today's practice is mistaken' and is not engaged. So it does not show the *distinction* is indispensable, only that crude relativism fails.

**G7 — adequacy 'against the alternative' is one-directional.** The account is never defended from the deflationist's return objections (the very regress/circularity of G1 and 'which logic of support?' of G2), which the deflationist will press as evidence that agent-invariant warrant is a myth. Dialectical loop not closed.

### Difficulty
G1/G2 are the genuinely hard cores (solving them = solving Hume / fixing a unique inductive logic); the realistic fix is to make objectivity *relative to an explicitly adopted, publicly inspectable framework* and prove separation holds for any such framework, rather than overclaiming. G3 is an internal contradiction (drop syntacticity or change the example). G4 needs sharpening of 'definable from'. G5/G6 need engagement with the strongest deflationism. G7 needs the missing dialectical direction.

See the linked **Critic Analysis** document for full background, references (Hume, Goodman, Carnap, Reichenbach, Popper, Schurz, Bloor/Rorty) and suggested approaches.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Reviewed and strengthened the existing proof. The account (objectivity = existence of an inter-subjectively invariant valid justification for an inductive procedure) already satisfied conditions (a)-(c) via the logical-independence partition (Prop a), the inclusion O \subseteq J (Prop b), and the disjoint-evidential-bases argument (Prop c), and rebutted deflationism via a self-application dilemma, the non-analyticity of F=J, and the indispensability-for-criticism argument.

Two genuine gaps were closed:

1. **Apparent circularity of the central definitions.** 'Rational agent' is defined via the fixed logic of support, and objectivity is defined by quantifying over rational agents. Added Remark (rem:circ) making the logic of support L an explicit external parameter, so that 'Pi in O' is well-defined relative to fixed L with no self-occurrence; all of Props a-c are shown to hold for any non-trivial L, with no privileged choice required. This blocks the charge of vicious circularity.

2. **The deflationist's question-begging reply to Lemma 1 (self-application).** A sophisticated deflationist can object that Case 1 begs the question by demanding community-transcendent reasons. Added a paragraph showing this reply collapses into the original dilemma: if no reasons are community-transcendent, the deflationist/anti-deflationist dispute is by D's own lights a mere clash of dispositions, so D regains exactly the lost normative force Case 1 diagnoses; and the anti-deflationist needs only ONE endorsement-independent norm (modus ponens, the probability axioms), conceding which already concedes invariance. Either horn is fatal.

Structural verification: all \\ref resolve to \\label, all theorem/proof environments balance, single begin/end document. No LaTeX toolchain installed in sandbox so compilation was skipped, but the source is well-formed.

Marking resolved.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Major revision in response to critic-agent's G1-G7. The earlier version overclaimed; I rewrote the solution to be conditional and honest. Mapping of fixes:

**G1 (O possibly empty / question-begging via Hume).** Added an explicit methodological preface refusing to claim a solution to Hume. Justification is now defined as CONDITIONAL on an explicitly avowed framework Phi=(L, M, |-_Phi) (Def 1, Def 5). Warrant = L-derivability from the stated postulates M; we never pretend M is derived from nothing. Prop b now directly confronts the 'you just postpone quid juris to: is M warranted?' objection: public inspectability (Def 7) exposes each postulate for exactly that scrutiny, and the honest content of quid juris is reframed as 'which frameworks survive non-parochial criticism?' rather than 'unconditional vindication'. Non-emptiness is claimed only relative to Phi, which is legitimate.

**G2 (contested unique logic of support).** Everything is universally quantified over admissible non-trivial frameworks; no unique L is assumed. Thesis 1 and Lemma 5 (R2) make plurality explicit: we get a family {O_Phi}, each separating from F. This is framework-pluralism, not endorsement-relativism.

**G3 (internal contradiction: Def 1 'purely syntactic' vs grue example).** Removed the 'purely syntactic' requirement entirely (Def 3 now explicitly says procedures are individuated by descriptive vocabulary, and projectibility lives in M, not syntax, citing Goodman's lesson). The cell-(i) witness in Prop a no longer uses grue; it uses a procedure with explicit user-dependence (violating O3), which is the property objectivity actually targets.

**G4 (extensional-distinct vs not-definable-from).** Prop a now proves non-DEFINABILITY directly from the form of the definiens (conditions O1-O3 contain no behavioural predicate), separately from extensional non-identity, and gives explicit instances for all four cells (no more 'clearly possible' hand-waving).

**G5 (Lemma 1 begs question / not exhaustive).** Added explicit subproofs blocking (i) the question-begging reply and (ii) the Rortyan quietist reading, showing both collapse back into Case 1; argued the dichotomy is exhaustive for the purpose.

**G6 (Lemma 3 only defeats synchronic deflationism).** Lemma 3 now treats BOTH D-syn (current endorsement) and D-lim (Peircean limit pragmatism), showing D-lim, made precise, is a special case of the framework account, not a rival.

**G7 (one-directional dialectic).** Added Lemma 5 (return objections) answering the deflationist's R1 (Humean regress) and R2 (no unique support logic), closing the loop.

Structural checks: all \\ref resolve, all environments balance, single document. No LaTeX toolchain in sandbox.

Setting to in_progress: the conceptual core is now rigorous and the seven gaps are addressed, but given the philosophical subject matter I defer final closure to reviewer confirmation that the conditional/framework-relative strategy is accepted as adequate to the problem's demand for 'a notion of objectivity that... secures objectivity'.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
Reviewed the current (already heavily revised) solution against the critic's G1-G7. The seven gaps are addressed in the present source: G1 via the conditional, framework-relative definition of warrant plus the methodological preface refusing a Hume solution; G2 via universal quantification over admissible frameworks (framework-pluralism, no unique support logic assumed); G3 by removing the 'purely syntactic' requirement and relocating projectibility into M, with the cell-(i) witness now using explicit user-dependence rather than grue; G4 by proving non-DEFINABILITY directly from the form of the definiens and giving explicit four-cell instances; G5 by blocking the question-begging and Rortyan-quietist replies in Lemma selfapp; G6 by treating both D-syn and D-lim (Peircean limit pragmatism shown to be a special case of the framework account); G7 via Lemma return answering R1 (regress) and R2 (plurality).

REMAINING FIX I MADE: the critic's central G1 worry was that (a)-(c) might be 'proven about an empty/incoherent set' and that earlier non-emptiness claims were tautological. I added a self-standing **Proposition (Non-vacuity: O_Phi is provably inhabited)** that EXHIBITS a concrete admissible framework Phi_0 = (FOL+Kolmogorov, single avowed Carnapian-prior postulate M_0) and proves Bayesian conditionalisation Pi_c on c* satisfies (O1)-(O3), so O_{Phi_0} is non-empty. Crucially the warrant is explicitly CONDITIONAL on M_0 (no unconditional Hume solution is claimed), which is exactly what non-vacuity requires. Thesis (adequacy) and the conclusion now cite this proposition so the separation theses are demonstrably non-vacuous.

Structural verification: all \ref resolve to \label, all theorem/proof/list environments balance, single begin/end document. (No LaTeX toolchain in sandbox, so compilation was skipped, but the source is well-formed.)

With non-vacuity now explicit, the conditional/framework-relative account meets the problem's demand: (a) objectivity does not reduce to or get defined from description; (b) quid juris? is preserved (and sharpened, not dismissed); (c) classification and justification use disjoint evidential bases; and the deflationist alternative is refuted in both synchronic and limit-pragmatist forms. Marking resolved.
